ul.li: Avoids lock-step synchronization with 100Hz application loops
Ensures statistical coverage of all execution phases
After ~100 samples: complete phase coverage guaranteed

div.p: App Loop: Every 10.0ms (100Hz) Profiler Sample: Every 10.1ms (99Hz) Drift: +0.1ms per sample

ul.li: Phase Coverage: 100% - every microsecond of the 10ms loop window sampled
Bias Eliminated: No consistent sampling at same execution point

div.p: Using a prime frequency (99Hz) ensures that samples never fall into a repeating pattern with the application's 100Hz loop. This provides unbiased statistical sampling of all execution phases.
Drift(n) = n × (T_app - T_sample) = n × (10ms - 10.101ms) = n × (-0.101ms) After 100 samples: Drift(100) = 100 × 0.101ms = 10.1ms ≈ 1 app cycle

ul.li: Practical coverage: 100 samples sufficient for statistical purposes
Each sample hits a unique phase modulo 10ms
